scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL JUDGE AT BENGALURU

ORIGINAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

BETWEEN:

RAM KUMAR, S/O {{FATHER'S NAME}}, AGED ABOUT 45 YEARS, R/O JAYANAGAR, BENGALURU, KARNATAKA – {{PINCODE}}. ... PLAINTIFF

AND:

SURESH PATEL, S/O {{FATHER'S NAME}}, AGED ABOUT 38 YEARS, R/O KORAMANGALA, BENGALURU, KARNATAKA – {{PINCODE}}. ... DEFENDANT

PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

1. The Plaintiff states that he is a resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru. The Defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru. Both parties are within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

2. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant is liable to pay the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) towards the principal amount advanced as a hand loan, along with interest thereon.

3. It is submitted that this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 9 and Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the Defendant resides within the jurisdiction of this Court and the cause of action arose within the limits of this Court.

4. The Plaintiff states that on 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant as a hand loan. The said amount was transferred via NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR: AXIB20240315123456 (as per Annexure-A). There was no written agreement executed between the parties regarding this loan.

5. Subsequent to the transfer, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount and the liability to repay the same via WhatsApp messages exchanged between the parties (as per Annexure-B).

6. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite the same falling due. The date of default for the purpose of interest calculation is 20.06.2024.

7. The Plaintiff sent a legal notice to the Defendant dated 01.07.2024 demanding repayment of the principal amount along with interest (as per Annexure-C). Despite the said notice, the Defendant has failed to repay the amount.

8. The cause of action for the present suit arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default in repayment, and subsequently on 01.07.2024 when the legal notice was issued and the Defendant failed to comply with the demand.

9. The suit is within the period of limitation as prescribed under the applicable Article of the Limitation Act, 1963, being within three years from the date the loan became due.

STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT

10. The Plaintiff submits the following statement of account regarding the dues from the Defendant:
(a) Principal Amount Advanced: Rs. 15,00,000/-
(b) Date of Advance: 15.03.2024
(c) Part Payments Received: Nil
(d) Balance Principal Due: Rs. 15,00,000/-
(e) Interest Claimed: 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 till realization.

INTEREST

11. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024. The Plaintiff claims interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for the period pending litigation and future interest till realization of the decretal amount.

COURT FEE

12. The Plaintiff states that the requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid on this plaint as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

PRAYER

13. The Plaintiff therefore prays for a judgment and decree against the Defendant for:
(a) Recovery of principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only);
(b) Interest at 12% per annum on Rs. 15,00,000/- from 20.06.2024 till date of filing;
(c) Pendente lite interest at 12% per annum under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
(d) Future interest at 12% per annum till realization of the decretal amount;
(e) Cost of the suit;
(f) Such other relief as this Hon'ble Court deems fit to grant in the interest of justice.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

14. The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents and lists them as Annexures:
ANNEXURE-A: Bank Record showing NEFT Transfer details with UTR: AXIB20240315123456 dated 15.03.2024.
ANNEXURE-B: Digital Communication comprising WhatsApp messages acknowledging the loan.
ANNEXURE-C: Legal Notice dated 01.07.2024 sent to the Defendant.

VERIFICATION

15. I, RAM KUMAR, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby verify and declare that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 14 of the plaint are true and correct to my knowledge and belief and nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at Bengaluru on this ___ day of ______ 2024.

PLAINTIFF

ADVOCATE FOR PLAINTIFF
{{ADVOCATE NAME}}
Enrollment No.: {{ENROLLMENT NUMBER}}
Address: {{OFFICE ADDRESS}}
Mobile: {{MOBILE NUMBER}}

citation_issues.message: 'Section 20 the Code' not in verified_provisions — may be correct but unverified
